title: The New Metabolic World Order and the Green Entente
content:
At the World Economic Forum in Davos, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney did not come to offer hope, but rather to pronounce a death. He announced that the liberal international order, built in the aftermath of World War II, was irreversibly over. However, his sober eulogy understated the true depth of the global rupture. The decline is not merely about ending diplomatic arrangements, but rather the conclusion of the fossil-fueled model of industrial civilization. This model made the old order possible, profitable, and politically sustainable for decades.

This decline was instigated by the manifest instability of climate change and rivalrous oil-access impediments. The resulting transition will not be a smooth negotiation in Geneva or The Hague. Instead, the replacement of the liberal international order will be determined by who controls critical energy flows, mineral deposits, and technological systems. This marks the beginning of an ecological cold war that differs enormously from its predecessor. While the original Cold War was a contest between liberal capitalism and Soviet communism, the new conflict is a battle between competing industrial metabolisms.

Opposing the traditional petrostates will be a new coalition known as the Green Entente. This emerging alliance seeks to dominate every link in the green industrial value chain. As middle powers struggle to adapt, the world is moving toward a new metabolic world order. Ultimately, the geopolitical balance will favor those who successfully navigate this transition. The nations that secure green energy supremacy will dictate the rules of the future global economy.
